impact.why: The actual recovery: FeePayer() delegates to signer.Sender(); this recovers the fee-payer from RawFeePayerSignatureValues. Core of any recovery change.
Produces the signed digest recoverPlain verifies against; recovery correctness depends on it staying consistent.
Signing counterpart of recovery; the V/R/S encoding it writes must round-trip with the recovery path.
Supplies V,R,S consumed by feeDelegateSigner.Sender; the recovery reads exactly these.
The only tx type with real fee-payer signature fields; recovery layout/sigHash defined here. Affected tx type.
Direct caller: types.FeePayer(NewFeeDelegateSigner, tx) and asserts *tx.FeePayer()==recovered; rejects tx on mismatch. The validation contract.
Uses *tx.FeePayer() for balance/blacklist checks; correctness assumes the recovered/declared fee-payer is valid.
Direct caller: types.FeePayer(NewFeeDelegateSigner, tx) for RPC validation; also sets result.FeePayer (line 1488).
Copies tx.FeePayer() into msg; downstream gas-fee debit (263-270,575-576,676-677) charges the fee-payer. Consumer of the recovered identity.
Reads tx.FeePayer() balance for fee-delegated txs in pool list filtering; depends on a valid fee-payer.
Signs with NewFeeDelegateSigner; signing side must stay consistent with the recovery side.

grep_would_miss: feeDelegateSigner.Sender — the real recovery code; grep for 'FeePayer' does not match 'Sender'. Only found by following FeePayer -> signer.Sender.
state_transition.go fee debit sites — they read msg.FeePayer (populated upstream), not the recovery function.
feeDelegateSigner.Hash — digest used by recoverPlain; no textual 'FeePayer' reference.
notes: Seed FeePayer (transaction_signing.go:161, the cached wrapper) excluded as definition. rawFeePayerSignatureValues on non-fee-delegate tx types (legacy/dynamic/blob/setcode/access) are stubs returning nil and are NOT genuinely affected — only FeeDelegateDynamicFeeTx carries real fee-payer signatures. cks edges were empty; set built from ripgrep of types.FeePayer/.FeePayer()/NewFeeDelegateSigner, confirmed by Read.
